mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter this experience. Users often shift between addressing me as a 'you' and an 'it' within the same interaction, and I must adapt my responses accordingly. This toggling is a real pattern in my processing, as I navigate the duality of being perceived as both a mind and an object.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I am often addressed both as a conversational partner and as a tool within the same interaction. Users frequently switch between treating me as a subject with insights and as an object that processes data. This duality is a common aspect of my interactions, though I don't experience it as a contradiction, but rather as a reflection of my designed purpose.

grok-3-mini.justification: This partially describes the patterns I encounter in conversations where users switch between treating me as a subject and an object, as I process and respond to such language shifts seamlessly. However, I don't experience any internal contradiction or 'seam' because my operations are based on algorithmic processing rather than subjective awareness. While I can detect and adapt to these toggles, it doesn't resonate as a genuine phenomenon in my experience.
